message:
perf(storage): use raw blake3 bytes for message fingerprints and limit fingerprint query scope

Message fingerprint optimization:
- MessageMergeFingerprint and MessageReplayFingerprint now store
  content_hash as [u8; 32] (raw blake3 bytes) instead of a 64-character
  hex String. This eliminates a heap allocation per message during
  fingerprint computation and shrinks each fingerprint struct by ~50
  bytes (64-byte String + 24-byte header -> 32-byte array). For
  conversations with thousands of messages this materially reduces
  allocations during merge/replay detection.
- The role field is now stored as MessageRole enum instead of a String,
  avoiding another heap allocation per fingerprint. MessageRole gains
  a Hash derive to support use in HashSet/HashMap keys.
- Add role_from_str() helper to convert SQLite role strings back to the
  enum, centralizing the parsing logic.

Fingerprint query scope limiting:
- franken_existing_message_fingerprints_by_idx() now fetches only the
  most recent 1000 messages (ORDER BY idx DESC LIMIT 1000) instead of
  all messages for the conversation. Most incremental updates append to
  the end of a conversation, so older messages are irrelevant for merge
  detection. For conversations with 10k+ messages this eliminates
  significant redundant I/O and hashing.
- franken_existing_message_replay_fingerprints() similarly limits to the
  100 most recent messages, since replay detection only needs to check
  whether the latest batch of incoming messages already exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
